tool_args.label: White Plains Station Renovations
tool_args.raw_text: MTA C&D is nearing completion of a gut rehabilitation of the third-busiest station on the Metro-North network – White Plains. Major improvements include: new elevators, stairs, heated platforms for ice control, bathrooms, ticket office, food vendor space, digital signage, and artwork.

payload.project_name: White Plains Station Renovations
payload.description: Gut rehabilitation of White Plains station, the third-busiest station on the Metro-North network. Improvements include new elevators, stairs, heated platforms, bathrooms, ticket office, food vendor space, digital signage, and artwork.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Rutgers Tunnel Repair and Station Improvements at East Broadway
tool_args.raw_text: MTA C&D is repairing the damage caused by Superstorm Sandy to the Rutgers Tunnel, which F trains use to cross the East River, and fortifying it against future flooding. This is the last of the MTA cross-river tunnels to be rehabbed through the Capital Program. The project also entails making significant station improvements at East Broadway. Work began in August 2020 and is expected to be completed in the fall of 2021. The project is being accelerated to a record 14 months to take advantage of the current period of low ridership.

payload.project_name: Rutgers Tunnel Repair
payload.description: Repair of Superstorm Sandy damage to the Rutgers Tunnel (used by F trains to cross the East River) and fortification against future flooding. Includes station improvements at East Broadway.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Modern Signals on the Queens Boulevard West Line (CBTC)
tool_args.raw_text: A large section of the Queens Boulevard Line, from 23 St-Ely Ave to 50 St-8 Ave and around 47-50 St Rockefeller Center, is now in CBTC revenue service. MTA C&D accelerated this phase of the project and avoided future outages by taking the line out of service for consecutive 9 days around the new year, when ridership is very low. When the project is complete, modern signals will allow for trains to run closer together and more reliably on this line, one of the busiest in the entire MTA network.

payload.project_name: Modern Signals on the Queens Boulevard West Line
payload.description: CBTC signal installation on the Queens Boulevard Line from 23 St-Ely Ave to 50 St-8 Ave and around 47-50 St Rockefeller Center. A section is now in CBTC revenue service.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: New Ramp to Connect the RFK Bridge to the Harlem River Drive
tool_args.raw_text: B&T constructed a new ramp connecting the Robert F. Kennedy Bridge to the Harlem River Drive, eliminating a detour through East Harlem. The new ramp removes up to 18,000 cars daily from local streets, in a community that suffers from air pollution and high asthma rates. This Design-Build project was completed in November 2020, one month ahead of schedule and under budget. Original budget: $72.6 M (including contingency) EAC: $55 M

payload.project_name: New Ramp to Connect the RFK Bridge to the Harlem River Drive
payload.description: Design-build project constructing a new ramp connecting the Robert F. Kennedy Bridge to the Harlem River Drive, eliminating detour through East Harlem.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Rehabilitation of the Skewbacks, Tower Piers, & Pedestals at the Henry Hudson Bridge
tool_args.raw_text: B&T rehabilitated the concrete skewbacks that support the steel arch of the bridge, and replaced the concrete pedestals that are the foundations for the piers that support the approach viaducts. This design-build project was completed in September, within the project budget. Original budget: $100.4 M; EAC: $100.4 M

payload.project_name: Rehabilitation of the Skewbacks, Tower Piers, & Pedestals at the Henry Hudson Bridge
payload.description: Rehabilitation of concrete skewbacks supporting the steel arch and replacement of concrete pedestals for pier foundations.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Reconstruction of the South Approach Roadway and Replacement of Facility Lighting System at the Henry Hudson Bridge
tool_args.raw_text: B&T replaced the original 1930's south approach roadways, including the substructure, superstructure and deck of the upper and lower levels of the Henry Hudson Bridge. This reconstruction replaced one of the last pieces of the original 1930's era bridge and enhanced safety and traffic flow by eliminating support columns on the lower level, improving sight distance and overall roadway alignment. The new structure also meets modern geometrics and seismic standards. In addition the Henry Hudson Parkway roadway lighting system was completely replaced. The project was completed in December 2020 within budget and one month ahead of schedule. Original budget: $109.6 M; EAC: $109.6

payload.project_name: Reconstruction of the South Approach Roadway and Replacement of Facility Lighting System at the Henry Hudson Bridge
payload.description: Replacement of original 1930's south approach roadways including substructure, superstructure and deck. Elimination of support columns on lower level. Replacement of Henry Hudson Parkway roadway lighting.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Rehabilitation of Tunnel Controls & Communication Systems at the Queens-Midtown Tunnel
tool_args.raw_text: B&T modernized tunnel controls and communication systems to improve customer safety in the Queens-Midtown Tunnel. This Design-Build project was completed in December, within budget and seven months ahead of schedule. Original budget: $39 M; EAC: $32.5 M

payload.project_name: Rehabilitation of Tunnel Controls & Communication Systems at the Queens-Midtown Tunnel
payload.description: Modernization of tunnel controls and communication systems for customer safety at the Queens-Midtown Tunnel.

tool_args.label: Installation of Electronic Monitoring & Detection Systems at the Bronx-Whitestone Bridge and the RFK Bridge
tool_args.raw_text: B&T installed new electronic traffic monitoring and fire detection systems at the BWB and RFK. The Design-Build project was completed in December, within budget and on schedule. Original budget: $88.3 M; EAC: $81 M

payload.project_name: Installation of Electronic Monitoring & Detection Systems at the Bronx-Whitestone Bridge and the RFK Bridge
payload.description: Installation of electronic traffic monitoring and fire detection systems at the Bronx-Whitestone Bridge and RFK Bridge.

tool_args.label: Two-way Tolling at the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge
tool_args.raw_text: B&T reconfigured the tolling area to enable two-way tolling at the VNB, eliminating an anomaly in regional traffic patterns created by federally mandated one-way tolling. The project was completed in November, within the project budget and one month ahead of schedule. Original budget: $39.6 M; EAC: $39.6 M

payload.project_name: Two-way Tolling at the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge
payload.description: Reconfiguration of tolling area to enable two-way tolling at the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Throgs Neck Bridge Orthotropic Deck Replacement (TN-49)
tool_args.raw_text: Project TN-49 is the staged replacement of the existing concrete deck with a new steel orthotropic deck. The orthotropic deck design will improve the bridge's wind performance, structural resiliency, and upgrade the suspended spans to meet current seismic and fatigue performance criteria. The contract was awarded November 2018 to Judlau Contracting Inc for $235M. The project's duration is 48 months and in addition to above deck replacement, the project's scope includes: Additional steel repairs to the supporting structure; Increasing deck cross slopes to improve drainage; Removal and replacement of four suspender ropes for testing; Main cable opening and inspection; Improving bridge utility systems.

payload.project_name: Throgs Neck Bridge Orthotropic Deck Replacement (TN-49)
payload.description: Staged replacement of existing concrete deck with new steel orthotropic deck to improve wind performance, structural resiliency, and meet seismic/fatigue criteria. Scope includes steel repairs, drainage improvements, suspender rope replacement, main cable inspection, and utility system improvements.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Phase 1 Reconstruction of VNB Upper Level Brooklyn and Staten Island Approach and Anchors Spans
tool_args.raw_text: Phase 1 of this project will replace the deck on the entire Brooklyn Approach and a portion of the Staten Island Approach. It will also replace the structural steel and decks of the Anchorages, and make critical repairs to the Belt Parkway Ramps. Status: Approx. 36% complete Original budget: $205M; EAC: $205M Substantial completion forecasted- June 2023 (on schedule)

payload.project_name: Phase 1 of the Reconstruction of the VNB Upper level Brooklyn and Staten Island Approach and Anchors Spans
payload.description: Replace deck on Brooklyn Approach and portion of Staten Island Approach. Replace structural steel and decks of Anchorages. Make critical repairs to Belt Parkway Ramps.

tool_args.label: Brooklyn Approach Reconstruction (Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge)
tool_args.raw_text: The on-grade Brooklyn Approach Reconstruction includes construction of a fourth lane to the Gowanus Expressway, which will alleviate a traffic bottleneck and improve customer safety. All roadway work was accelerated under the Moving NY Forward Initiative and the fourth travel lane was opened to traffic in October 2020, 2 months ahead of the original project schedule. Status: Approx. 90% complete Original budget: $26.1M   EAC: $26.1M Substantial completion forecasted: June 2021 (ahead of the original planned December 2021 completion date)

payload.project_name: Brooklyn Approach Reconstruction
payload.description: On-grade approach reconstruction including construction of a fourth lane to the Gowanus Expressway to alleviate a traffic bottleneck. Fourth travel lane opened in October 2020.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Throgs Neck Bridge - Replacement of the Suspended Span Deck
tool_args.raw_text: This project is to reconstruct the roadway including replacing the original 1960's concrete grid deck with a lightweight steel orthotropic deck, along with performing steel repairs and painting of the steel supporting the deck; upgrading the bridge roadway lighting and bridge electrical systems, and installing a fire standpipe system. In addition, the project includes removing and replacing several suspender ropes and main cable investigation to determine the current load carrying capacity of the main cables. The deck work is being accomplished in six stages using a movable barrier to maintain traffic capacity in the peak direction. Project benefits include extending the service life of the bridge by reducing the load on the main cables as well as improving seismic and wind performance and overall structural resiliency. Status: Approx. 43% complete Original budget: $336M; EAC: $336M Substantial completion forecasted: November 2022 (on target)

payload.project_name: Replacement of the Suspended Span Deck (Throgs Neck Bridge)
payload.description: Reconstruct roadway replacing original 1960's concrete grid deck with lightweight steel orthotropic deck, steel repairs, painting, upgrading roadway lighting, bridge electrical systems, installing fire standpipe system, removing/replacing suspender ropes, main cable investigation.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Rehabilitation of the TNB Viaducts
tool_args.raw_text: This project is to repair the steel structure supporting the roadway and the concrete piers. Work is being performed from a work platform under the structure. This project has minimal traffic impacts. Status: Approx. 18% complete Original budget: $212.2M; EAC: $212.2 M Substantial completion forecasted: August 2023 (on target)

payload.project_name: Rehabilitation of the TNB Viaducts
payload.description: Repair of the steel structure supporting the roadway and the concrete piers on the Throgs Neck Bridge viaducts.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Henry Hudson Bridge Structural Rehabilitation and Painting
tool_args.raw_text: As discussed above under 2020 Major Completions, the HHB Program has already completed the Skewback Rehabilitation and the South Approach Reconstruction. Under construction now is the structural rehabilitation and painting of the steel arch and approach structures. This project has minimal traffic impacts. Status: Approx. 46% complete Original budget: $58.8M; EAC: $58.9M Substantial completion forecasted: March 2022 (on target)

payload.project_name: Henry Hudson Bridge Structural Rehabilitation and Painting
payload.description: Structural rehabilitation and painting of the steel arch and approach structures at the Henry Hudson Bridge.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: RFK Bridge Structural Rehabilitation of Viaducts
tool_args.raw_text: The focus of the program now turns to necessary rehabilitation and retrofits needed on the viaducts and the suspended span to extend the service life of the existing structures. The structural rehabilitation of the viaducts is currently ongoing, with work being performed to repair and upgrade the steel supporting the roadway to meet current load criteria. Status: Approx. 37% complete Original budget: $85 M; EAC: $85 M Substantial completion forecasted: August 2022 (on target)

payload.project_name: RFK Bridge Structural Rehabilitation of Viaducts
payload.description: Structural rehabilitation of viaducts to repair and upgrade steel supporting the roadway to meet current load criteria.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Moving NY Forward Program (C&D Acceleration - B&T)
tool_args.raw_text: Moving NY Forward Program (C&D Acceleration) - B&T advanced $144 M of work in 2020 to be performed while traffic volumes were reduced. To date, $85.5 M of the Moving NY Forward initiatives have been completed with another $48.5 M expected to be completed in 2021.

payload.project_name: Moving NY Forward Program (C&D Acceleration)
payload.description: B&T advanced $144 M of work in 2020 to be performed while traffic volumes were reduced. To date, $85.5 M of the Moving NY Forward initiatives have been completed with another $48.5 M expected to be completed in 2021.

tool_args.label: VNB Safety Fence Installation (Upper and Lower Level Suspended Spans)
tool_args.raw_text: Also at the VNB, the design-build project for installation of a safety fence on the upper and lower level suspended spans is currently under procurement, and is scheduled to be presented at the March Board for approval. This project has garnered a great deal of public interest, and is a key public safety project.

payload.project_name: VNB Safety Fence
payload.description: Design-build project for installation of a safety fence on the upper and lower level suspended spans of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge. Currently under procurement, scheduled to be presented at March Board for approval.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: HHB Facility Substation Upgrade (Design-Build)
tool_args.raw_text: In addition to performing structural repairs, B&T is completing preliminary design for an upcoming design-build project to upgrade and replace substations that provide primary and back-up power for the facility to ensure full electrical redundancy for the entire Henry Hudson Facility. The design is currently 10% complete, the DB RFP is under preparation and the project will be advertised this year.

payload.project_name: HHB Substation Upgrade
payload.description: Preliminary design for upgrade and replacement of substations providing primary and back-up power for the Henry Hudson facility to ensure full electrical redundancy.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: RFK Bridge Long-Lead Design-Build Projects
tool_args.raw_text: In addition, preliminary design is progressing for two critical design-build projects planned for later in the 2020-2024 program: the Structural Rehabilitation and Wind Retrofit of the suspended spans, and Rehabilitation of the Anchorage Structures. Both designs are currently less than 5% complete and are progressing on schedule.

payload.project_name: RFK Bridge Structural Rehabilitation and Wind Retrofit / Anchorage Rehabilitation
payload.description: Preliminary design progressing for two design-build projects: Structural Rehabilitation and Wind Retrofit of the suspended spans, and Rehabilitation of the Anchorage Structures.
